description: High-aggression architecture campaign ideas for the separate Codex autoresearch loop.
variant_rule_default: Each attempt must implement the next blueprint as a materially different architecture redesign, not a local refinement ladder.

ideas.title: XSA + Neural Cache
ideas.goal: Implement real XSA or top-layer cross-window KV cache branches that change the carrier and evaluation behavior enough to test the long-context hypothesis honestly.
forbidden_refinements: carrier-only preparation without real XSA or cache behavior
path cleanup or canonicalization without a new cache or XSA variant
same carrier with only a late-tail precision reshuffle
hints: Prefer 11-12 layer 512-dim carriers instead of polishing the current depth-3 branch.
Top-layer cache is preferred before whole-model cache.
At least half of the attempts should introduce real XSA or cache logic in train.py.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: Top-Tail XSA Carrier
attempt_blueprints.summary: Replace the branch-tip carrier with an 11-layer 512-dim architecture that keeps a simple lower stack but turns the final 3-4 layers into real XSA blocks with partial RoPE, seq_len 2048, and no TTT.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Top-Layer Prefix Cache
attempt_blueprints.summary: Build a 12-layer 512-dim XSA tail variant that adds a top-layer cross-window prefix KV cache or summary cache for only the last 2-3 layers while keeping causal scoring intact.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: Low-Rank-Q XSA Hybrid
attempt_blueprints.summary: Use low-rank Q to fund a 12-layer carrier with top-3 XSA layers plus a lighter cache path, explicitly spending the Q savings on extra depth rather than same-carrier polish.

attempt_blueprints.label: D
attempt_blueprints.title: Shared Trunk Plus Unique XSA Tail
attempt_blueprints.summary: Switch to a shared-trunk-plus-unique-tail layout where the upper 4 unique tail layers are XSA-only and the lower shared stack stays conventional, still targeting seq_len 2048.

attempt_blueprints.label: E
attempt_blueprints.title: 1536-to-2048 XSA Curriculum
attempt_blueprints.summary: Run an 11-layer XSA-tail branch with a 1536 to 2048 curriculum and lower batch tokens so the XSA story tests longer-context growth rather than just module insertion.

attempt_blueprints.label: F
attempt_blueprints.title: Cache-Heavy Upper Tail
attempt_blueprints.summary: Try the most aggressive still-plausible cache branch: 11-12 layers, 512-dim, top-layer cache plus XSA in the final tail, accepting that the run may fail or score poorly if the mechanism is wrong.

ideas.title: Late Selective Compression-Aware Quantization
ideas.goal: Explore coarse-group late-QAT and PTQ-style redesigns on stronger 11-12 layer carriers rather than slicing tiny float sets on the existing branch tip.
forbidden_refinements: repeated slicing of the same late-tail float set
single-tensor precision toggles on an unchanged carrier
same-carrier fake-quant step nudges without a coarse-group redesign
hints: Think in groups like head and embeddings, early attention, late attention, middle MLPs, and final MLPs.
Use later QAT or PTQ selection, not blanket early QAT.
Expect wider score spread than the ordinary loop.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: No-QAT PTQ Baseline Carrier
attempt_blueprints.summary: Build an 11-layer 512-dim near-cap carrier with no train-time QAT at all, denser warmdown checkpoints, and a post-quant selection path so the attempt actually tests the PTQ side of the hypothesis.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Boundary-Safe Late-QAT
attempt_blueprints.summary: Use a 12-layer 512-dim carrier where only middle MLPs and late attention enter QAT late in training, while embeddings, head, and final MLPs stay safer.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: Head-Emb Safe With Late Attention QAT
attempt_blueprints.summary: Try an 11-layer carrier that keeps embeddings and output head in higher precision, turns on QAT only for late attention plus middle MLPs near the final few percent of training, and rebalances depth accordingly.

attempt_blueprints.label: D
attempt_blueprints.title: PTQ-Soup Quant Branch
attempt_blueprints.summary: Run a no-QAT or minimal-QAT branch that saves several warmdown checkpoints and chooses the best post-quant result or a tiny soup after quantization, even if float loss is not the best.

attempt_blueprints.label: E
attempt_blueprints.title: Late-QAT With 12-Layer Carrier
attempt_blueprints.summary: Spend the quantization budget on a full 12-layer 512-dim carrier with late-only coarse QAT groups, using the stronger carrier as the main change instead of another precision micro-carve-out.

attempt_blueprints.label: F
attempt_blueprints.title: High-Risk Coarse Group Sweep
attempt_blueprints.summary: Try the largest still-plausible coarse-group redesign: boundary-safe precision, middle MLP int6 or int5 late QAT, and a different carrier partition, accepting that this may score much worse if the sensitivity map is wrong.

ideas.title: Low-Rank Q Structural Reallocation
ideas.goal: Use low-rank Q to fund obvious macro reallocations like extra depth, stronger local modules, or more steps instead of tiny same-carrier reallocations.
forbidden_refinements: same-carrier tail-width nudges funded by small Q cuts
adding another copy of the same local module on the same outer carrier
low-rank Q without a meaningful spend of the savings
hints: Rank 128 or 192 style reallocations should buy something obvious.
Prefer 11-12 layer 512-dim carriers or a different module family.
Poor scores are acceptable if the redesign is real.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: Low-Rank-Q Extra Depth
attempt_blueprints.summary: Use low-rank Q to build a 12-layer 512-dim carrier with more unique depth than the accepted branch, explicitly spending the savings on an extra layer rather than another tail polish.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Low-Rank-Q Plus Smarter Local Module
attempt_blueprints.summary: Use low-rank Q to fund a different local-token module family on an 11-layer carrier, replacing the current neighbor-mixer family instead of duplicating it.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: Low-Rank-Q More-Steps Branch
attempt_blueprints.summary: Convert Q savings into a lower batch or higher grad-accum regime on a near-cap 11-12 layer carrier so the attempt really tests more updates rather than same-carrier width.

attempt_blueprints.label: D
attempt_blueprints.title: Low-Rank-Q XSA Spend
attempt_blueprints.summary: Spend low-rank Q savings on introducing top-tail XSA or a similarly new upper-tail mechanism, not on the existing local mixer family.

attempt_blueprints.label: E
attempt_blueprints.title: Asymmetric Q-Rank Carrier
attempt_blueprints.summary: Try an asymmetric low-rank-Q topology where lower or shared layers stay compressed but the upper tail keeps fuller Q, using the saved bytes for a different 11-12 layer partition.

attempt_blueprints.label: F
attempt_blueprints.title: Largest Plausible Q Reallocation
attempt_blueprints.summary: Take the largest still-plausible low-rank-Q swing: new rank topology, new carrier partition, and a different spend target such as depth or module family, even if it risks a much worse score.

ideas.title: Split Local-Context Families
ideas.goal: Treat XSA-family and SmearGate or TTT-family designs as separate branch families and establish them cleanly instead of blurring them into one hybrid line.
forbidden_refinements: hybrids that blur XSA and TTT without evidence
cleanup-only commits inside an existing family
smear or TTT attempts that only polish the current neighbor path
hints: Three attempts should be clear XSA-family branches; three should be clear SmearGate or TTT-family branches.
Do not stack both families in a single early attempt.
Use the carrier to clarify the family identity.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: Pure XSA Family Carrier
attempt_blueprints.summary: Build a clean XSA-family branch with 11-12 layers, top-tail XSA, EMA or partial RoPE style shaping, and explicitly no TTT or SmearGate.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Pure SmearGate Family Carrier
attempt_blueprints.summary: Build a separate SmearGate-family branch with a stronger local module and mixed low-bit plan, explicitly with no XSA in the upper tail.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: XSA Family With Late QAT
attempt_blueprints.summary: Try a clearer XSA-family carrier that adds late selective QAT and context 2048, still without any TTT or SmearGate mechanics.

attempt_blueprints.label: D
attempt_blueprints.title: Smear Plus Meta-TTT Family
attempt_blueprints.summary: Try a SmearGate branch paired with a lightweight meta-TTT or causal-TTT style mechanism and no XSA, even if this requires writing a new module surface.

attempt_blueprints.label: E
attempt_blueprints.title: XSA Family With Low-Rank-Q Spend
attempt_blueprints.summary: Use low-rank Q or another obvious structural spend to create a stronger XSA-family branch that stays cleanly separate from the TTT family.

attempt_blueprints.label: F
attempt_blueprints.title: Smear Family With Better Local Module
attempt_blueprints.summary: Take the strongest still-plausible SmearGate-family redesign: new local-token module, mixed low-bit branch, maybe lightweight TTT, but still no XSA.

ideas.title: Compute-Aware Context Curriculum
ideas.goal: Use real shorter-to-longer context curricula paired with carrier or batch changes rather than small context nudges on the current depth-3 branch.
forbidden_refinements: warmup-only tweaks on the same curriculum shape
same-carrier context nudges without a compute trade
curricula that never meaningfully change the carrier
hints: Think 1024 or 1536 to 2048, not just 640 to 768.
Spend compute changes on more updates or a stronger carrier.
Expect some obvious losers.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: 1024-to-2048 11-Layer Curriculum
attempt_blueprints.summary: Switch to an 11-layer 512-dim carrier with a real 1024 to 2048 curriculum and lower batch tokens so the experiment actually reclaims steps early.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: 1536-to-2048 Late Curriculum
attempt_blueprints.summary: Try a 12-layer carrier that trains at 1536 for most of the run and only pays for 2048 late, explicitly using the saved compute for a stronger upper tail.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: 1024-1536-2048 Staged Carrier
attempt_blueprints.summary: Run a three-stage context curriculum on an 11-layer carrier with a batch schedule that materially changes update count, not just another context toggle.

attempt_blueprints.label: D
attempt_blueprints.title: Low-Rank-Q Curriculum Spend
attempt_blueprints.summary: Use low-rank Q or a smaller local module to pay for a stronger 1024 or 1536 to 2048 curriculum on a 12-layer carrier.

attempt_blueprints.label: E
attempt_blueprints.title: XSA-Aware Curriculum
attempt_blueprints.summary: Combine a real longer-context curriculum with an XSA-tail carrier so the context branch tests whether the mechanism benefits from late long-context exposure.

attempt_blueprints.label: F
attempt_blueprints.title: Largest Plausible Curriculum Swing
attempt_blueprints.summary: Take the biggest still-plausible curriculum redesign with real batch and carrier changes, accepting a poor score if the longer-context compute trade is wrong.

ideas.title: Smarter Local-Token Module
ideas.goal: Replace crude BigramHash-style local signal with new compact local-token modules rather than polishing the current neighbor path.
forbidden_refinements: neighbor-mixer cleanup without a new module family
same-module width or precision polish
reusing the current local module with only placement tweaks
hints: The module family should visibly change between attempts.
Top-N exact plus hashed tail, two-scale local features, and factorized projections are all fair game.
Use compact modules that plausibly fit the submission constraints.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: Exact-Plus-Hash Bigram Module
attempt_blueprints.summary: Introduce a compact top-N exact bigram table plus hashed tail module on an 11-layer 512-dim carrier, replacing the current local path rather than refining it.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Two-Scale Adjacent Plus Skip Module
attempt_blueprints.summary: Write a two-scale local feature module that mixes adjacent bigrams and skip-bigrams on a stronger 11-12 layer carrier.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: Factorized Bigram Projection
attempt_blueprints.summary: Replace the current local token path with a factorized bigram projection module that aims for a better byte-quality tradeoff than a monolithic table.

attempt_blueprints.label: D
attempt_blueprints.title: Lower-Stack Local Module
attempt_blueprints.summary: Try a redesigned local-token module placed low in an 11-12 layer carrier instead of only near the final tail, so the attempt meaningfully changes the stack topology.

attempt_blueprints.label: E
attempt_blueprints.title: Upper-Tail Local Module With XSA Tail
attempt_blueprints.summary: Pair a new local-token module family with an XSA-style upper tail on a 12-layer carrier so the attempt probes a genuinely different stack than the current branch.

attempt_blueprints.label: F
attempt_blueprints.title: Strongest Byte-Quality Local Module
attempt_blueprints.summary: Take the largest still-plausible local-token redesign with a new module family and new placement, even if the run risks a visibly worse score.

ideas.title: Quantization-Optimized Checkpoint Soup
ideas.goal: Test real post-quant checkpoint selection and soup logic on strong carriers instead of only nudging QAT schedules.
forbidden_refinements: tiny schedule polish on unchanged post-quant selection logic
checkpoint-saving changes without a real post-quant selection target
hints: At least some attempts should disable QAT and rely on post-quant ranking.
Dense warmdown checkpoint saves are expected here.
A weak float checkpoint can still be a good quantized checkpoint.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: No-QAT Post-Quant Selection
attempt_blueprints.summary: Run an 11-layer 512-dim carrier with no QAT, denser warmdown checkpoint saves, and explicit post-quant checkpoint selection.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Top-2 Post-Quant Soup
attempt_blueprints.summary: Run a similar strong carrier but soup the top two checkpoints by post-quant validation instead of selecting a single best float checkpoint.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: Selective SWA Plus PTQ
attempt_blueprints.summary: Test a carrier that uses selective late SWA over the final warmdown portion plus PTQ-style ranking of resulting checkpoints.

attempt_blueprints.label: D
attempt_blueprints.title: 12-Layer PTQ-Soup Carrier
attempt_blueprints.summary: Spend the same idea on a full 12-layer carrier with post-quant snapshot ranking so the attempt measures whether stronger carriers benefit more from soup selection.

attempt_blueprints.label: E
attempt_blueprints.title: Low-Rank-Q PTQ Branch
attempt_blueprints.summary: Use low-rank Q or another structural spend to fit a stronger carrier, then select or soup checkpoints by post-quant quality rather than float loss.

attempt_blueprints.label: F
attempt_blueprints.title: Most Aggressive Soup Trial
attempt_blueprints.summary: Take the largest still-plausible checkpoint-soup redesign with denser saves and post-quant ranking, accepting a poor score if the selection logic is wrong.

ideas.title: Localized Canon Inserts
ideas.goal: Write localized Canon-style neighbor-mixing inserts and place them in meaningfully different carriers rather than reusing the current neighbor path.
forbidden_refinements: canonicalization or cleanup of an existing neighbor path
reusing the same placement without a materially new Canon mechanism
same carrier with only tiny local-module toggles
hints: These should be real Canon-style inserts, not renamed neighbor polish.
Lower-stack or pre-final-XSA placements are preferred.
Keep the mechanism localized and interpretable.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: Bottom Canon Insert
attempt_blueprints.summary: Add a single localized Canon-style insert low in an 11-layer 512-dim carrier so the lower stack, not the tail, changes meaningfully.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Pre-Final-XSA Canon Insert
attempt_blueprints.summary: Pair a Canon insert right before a top-tail XSA block in a 12-layer carrier, keeping the Canon change local and interpretable.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: Dual Canon Inserts
attempt_blueprints.summary: Try two localized Canon inserts at distinct depths in a stronger carrier to test whether Canon behaves better as a sparse pattern than a single late add-on.

attempt_blueprints.label: D
attempt_blueprints.title: Canon Replacing Current Local Path
attempt_blueprints.summary: Remove or bypass the current neighbor-style local path and replace it with a Canon-style insert family on an 11-layer carrier.

attempt_blueprints.label: E
attempt_blueprints.title: Low-Rank-Q Canon Carrier
attempt_blueprints.summary: Use low-rank Q or a smaller attention budget to fit a cleaner Canon branch on a 12-layer carrier rather than bolting Canon onto the current winner.

attempt_blueprints.label: F
attempt_blueprints.title: Largest Plausible Canon Redesign
attempt_blueprints.summary: Take the biggest still-plausible localized Canon swing with a different carrier shape and placement, accepting that it may score much worse if Canon is a dead end here.

ideas.title: Selective INT4 MLP Moonshot
ideas.goal: Test genuinely different selective INT4 MLP carriers where INT4 frees capacity for depth or new modules instead of becoming another same-carrier precision slice.
forbidden_refinements: blanket same-carrier precision slicing without a new selective INT4 target pattern
INT4 attempts that do not spend the savings on a macro change
hints: Keep boundary and final MLPs at safer precision.
Middle-layer MLPs are the main targets.
These attempts can and should be volatile.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: Middle-Two INT4 With Extra Depth
attempt_blueprints.summary: Quantize only the middle two or three MLPs to INT4 on an 11-layer carrier and spend the savings on extra unique depth or stronger upper-tail layers.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Middle-Band INT4 With 12 Layers
attempt_blueprints.summary: Use selective middle-band INT4 to fit a 12-layer 512-dim carrier while keeping boundary and final MLPs safer.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: Lower-Middle INT4 Plus Better Local Module
attempt_blueprints.summary: Target lower-middle MLPs for INT4 and spend the saved bytes on a different local-token module family rather than the current local path.

attempt_blueprints.label: D
attempt_blueprints.title: INT4 Plus Low-Rank-Q Carrier
attempt_blueprints.summary: Combine selective middle-layer INT4 with low-rank Q to fund a meaningfully stronger carrier or more updates, not a same-carrier precision tweak.

attempt_blueprints.label: E
attempt_blueprints.title: INT4 Middle Band With XSA Tail
attempt_blueprints.summary: Spend selective INT4 savings on an XSA-style upper tail so the attempt becomes a genuine architecture branch rather than a pure quantization test.

attempt_blueprints.label: F
attempt_blueprints.title: Highest-Risk INT4 Moonshot
attempt_blueprints.summary: Take the largest still-plausible selective INT4 redesign with clear macro spend of the savings, accepting that a bad `1.60+` score is useful telemetry if the moonshot fails.
